gpu: AMD Radeon RX 6800 XT (radeonsi, navi21, LLVM 21.1.8, DRM 3.64, 6.18.7-200.nobara.fc43.x86_64)
notes:
Status: Unplayable / Crashing
System Information:

OS: Nobara Linux 43 (Fedora-based)

Kernel: 6.18.7-200.nobara.fc43.x86_64

GPU: AMD Radeon RX 6800 XT (Mesa 25.3.4 / RADV)

Display Server: Tested both Wayland and X11 (GNOME)

Issue Description: The game crashes immediately after the Easy Anti-Cheat (EAC) splash screen with an "Unexpected Error" and generates a THEIA minidump file. The game never reaches the main menu.

Troubleshooting Steps Taken (None of these worked):

Proton Versions: Tested with Proton 9.0-3, Proton Experimental, Proton Hotfix, and GE-Proton 10-29.

Launch Options: Tried various combinations including -dx11, -dx12, PROTON_ENABLE_SECCOMP=0, VKD3D_CONFIG=no_raytracing, and RADV_PERFTEST=rt.

Kernel & System Tweaks: * Disabled split lock mitigation (sudo sysctl -w kernel.split_lock_mitigate=0).

Adjusted network MTU to 1450.

Switched from Wayland to X11 session.

Increased vm.max_map_count to 1048576.

Maintenance: Deleted compatdata (prefix 1808500) multiple times, verified game files, and ensured "Proton EasyAntiCheat Runtime" was installed and updated.

Technical Findings from Logs: Proton logs are flooded with thousands of SIGSYS errors (seh:sigsys_handler) and trace:seh:install_bpf calls. This indicates a fundamental conflict where the game or its Anti-Cheat implementation is attempting system calls that are being blocked or improperly handled by the current combination of Proton and the Linux kernel security filters (seccomp).

title: ARC Raiders

notes:
I've been getting a generic UE Process crash whenever trying to launch ARC Raiders since Tuesday, February 17th, 2026. The game crashes during or just after the photo-sensitivity warning, and the only details I get from the crash is "You do not have any debugging symbols required to display the callstack for this crash."

What have I tried so far? Well, quite a lot. Here are some of them:

1. Added `vsyscall=emulate` and `clearcpuid=514` arguments to limine.conf
2. Disabled Integrated Graphics in Gigabyte BIOS and confirmed that 4070 Ti SUPER was the only device listed under `vulkaninfo --summary`
3. Added the following environments to my hyprland.conf:
```
env = LIBVA_DRIVER_NAME,nvidia`
env = __GLX_VENDOR_LIBRARY_NAME,nvidia
env = VK_ICD_FILENAMES,/usr/share/vulkan/icd.d/nvidia_icd.json
```
4. A variety of Steam launch options, the latest of which being: `WINEDLLOVERRIDES="preloader=n,b;nvapi,nvapi64=d" PROTON_ENABLE_NGX_UPDATER=1 %command% -windowed -noborder`
5. I've deleted the compatdata
6. I've removed the .utoc files and verified the game files
7. I've uninstalled and reinstalled
8. I even switched to a different Linux distro! (was on Pop!_OS and hated it, so the change was needed anyway)

It feels like this is related to the tiny update that went out yesterday in Build 21957403 (https://steamdb.info/patchnotes/21957403/#changed-files), but I cannot confirm.
